title: A typology of activities over a century of urban growth

meta_data.Data_summary: District-level population census dataset for Paris used to provide the time series of Paris population against which directory-based activity counts are scaled and to contextualize population change alongside administrative boundary changes.
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: The dataset of the population censuses of Paris at the scale of districts between 1801 and 1911 is openly accessible , Location: Data availability, Confidence: high

meta_data.Data_Name: Paris directories entries with NAICS inspired categories 1829 to 1907
meta_data.Data_summary: Geohistorical microdata derived from Paris city directories where each record represents a person or sometimes an organization linked to an economic activity and a street address, then cleaned and grouped into NAICS-inspired activity categories to analyze activity counts, ranks, and scaling over time.
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: The dataset of Paris directories entries with NAICS-inspired categories between 1829 and 1907 specifically constructed and used for this paper is openly accessible on the Zenodo platform https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.8388101 ref. 68 , Location: Data availability, Confidence: high

meta_data.Data_Name: Annuaires historiques parisiens 1798 to 1914 structured geolocated directory entries
meta_data.Data_summary: Large-scale structured and geolocated digitization of Paris historical directories produced via an automated pipeline including OCR, named-entity recognition, semantic enrichment, and historical geocoding, serving as the upstream source from which the paper selects and analyzes entries for 1829–1907.
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: More than 10 million entries currently compose the 141 digitally transcribed and enriched directories ref. 17 v.3 alphabetical lists , Location: Methods Paris directories between 1799 and 1914, Confidence: high
